post_rendered:
Mafia Game 224!
Suspectum Industries

  • Rules
    • Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • Do not post at night.
    • You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • Scum chat will be via QuickTopic and there is no day chat.
    • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, Zwjs text, among others.
    • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • To confirm, after receiving your role pm reply to it with your role name.
    • If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • Do not imitate the mods.
  • Voting/Hanging
    • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". Type usernames exactly. Two exceptions, the first being capitalization in the username does not matter, the second being i and L are treated as the same.
    • Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang".
    • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though you have the ability to do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • If the deadline is met the player with the most votes is hanged. If there is a tie the player who received the votes first is hanged. If No Hang is part of the tie, No Hang occurs regardless.
    • Starting day 1, If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • Starting day 1, If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • The moment the deadline passes, it will be twilight and no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • Once I (or the comod) post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • Each day will be 240 hours (10 days). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion.
    • The deadlines will be based on Central Timezone.
    • The deadline will occur the very second 240 hours has occurred.
    • The night deadline will be 48 hours after night begins.
    • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from a player the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night. In the case of the mafia's mandatory nightkill, a townie will be selected at random.
    • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • If a player does not post in another 24-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, or a player has received too many PM prods, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • Do not use inappropriate language or make personal attacks.
    • Be mature.
    • The game is to not be considered over until the mod or co-mod declares it as such.
    • Don't intentionally/purposely gamethrow. If you believe you can no longer win talk to me in your role pm.
    • For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she may be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • I (NDMath) am the moderator of Mafia Game 224. Mathletesv is the comod of Mafia Game 224.
    • This brown (#974E03) is my mod color. Do not post in anything too similar. This blue (#4d94ff) is Mathletesv's mod color. Do not post in anything too similar.
    • The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules. The co-mod the second to last say.

  • Read all of the rules
Setup
Setup is Open.

N1 Vigilante
N2 Vigilante
N3 Vigilante
Vanilla Townie * 6

Mafia Goon * 3

Mafia nightkill is mandatory, vigilante kills are not.
Mafia chat is via quicktopic, they do not have daychat.

Role PM's
N1 Vigilante wrote:
You are the First Board Official (Town N1 Vigilante) of Suspectum Industries. It has been so long since the last board meeting that you're not really sure who the other board officials even are. As a board official, you are able to take matters into your own hands, and during Night 1 you may anonymously fire (kill) another player.

You win once when none of the Saboteurs (Mafia) are present (alive).
N2 Vigilante wrote:
You are the Second Board Official (Town N2 Vigilante) of Suspectum Industries. It has been so long since the last board meeting that you're not really sure who the other board officials even are. As a board official, you are able to take matters into your own hands, and during Night 2 you may anonymously fire (kill) another player.

You win once when none of the Saboteurs (Mafia) are present (alive).
N3 Vigilante wrote:
You are the Third Board Official (Town N3 Vigilante) of Suspectum Industries. It has been so long since the last board meeting that you're not really sure who the other board officials even are. As a board official, you are able to take matters into your own hands, and during Night 3 you may anonymously fire (kill) another player.

You win once when none of the Saboteurs (Mafia) are present (alive).
Vanilla Townie wrote:
You are a Manager (Vanilla Townie) of Suspectum Industries. As a loyal manager of Suspectum Industries you would hate to see its collapse.

You when once when none of the Saboteurs (Mafia) are present (alive).
Mafia Goon wrote:
You are a Professional Saboteur (Mafia Goon). You and your buddies {Username} (Mafia Goon) and {Username} (Mafia Goon) aim to take down Suspectum Industries. Each night the three of you must select a player to sabotage, leading to them being fired for their poor work.

You when once when the Saboteurs (Mafia) control at least half of the present (alive) positions.

Notable Stuff

post_rendered:
Ok, so majority seems to agree with me.

We essentially have 2 hangs everyday. So everyone will vote for who they think is scum, but they'll also have a secondary hang. We'll do a boatcount then, for who should be vigged. So we hang whoever has the most votes for the hang and vig whoever has the most votes in the boatcount.

Exactly 24 hours before EOD, the person winning the boatcount will claim. If they claim VT, we vig them. If they claim vig (with night), we wait for ccs, and if there are none, then we quickly decide on another vigshot. If there's a cc we decide who's scum and who's vig UNLESS there's a N1 vig cc, in which case we just do nothing and let mafia and vig kill each other.

At MYLO/LYLO, all the vigs remaining claim.

@bigmath

since technically the people who claim vt should be the ones that we don't shoot
shush

post_rendered:
I propose the following statement: Vigs should never holster unless it is a situation where if they do shoot they have a chance at losing the game. (e.g. we mishanged a VT D1, Vig N1 made a shot on a VT, scum made a shot on a VT, we mishanged a VT on D2, if vig shoots and misses then we lose.)

N1 Vig should always shoot imo given that if the VT's and the vig's make a decent PoE then scum will be on their feet for today. We kinda want that pressure and even if the N1 Vig thinks he is bad at shooting and stuff just make a rand shot in the PoE.

Still questioning to whether all Vig's should claim. I feel like it's a little too early to let them claim now for sure? Maybe if we let them all claim at the end of the day to spread scum's kill potential out and make sure the N1 doesn't randomly shoot a vig in PoE. Clearly the named townies are a boost but claiming right at RVS start is kinda bruh because that basically sets scum's kill potential on the vig's only and not on fearkills or anything else. I think we still claim vig today given that named townies could really help us in future days (scum can only kill 1 vig n1) but if someone like Osmium gets good reads and has massive fearkill potential then we can capitalize on that at the end of the day.

Counterclaims:

Clearly if you are under pressure like L-1 on your wagon you should claim with your night if you are a vig. That allows potential CC to happen.

Do not be afraid to counterclaim with your night if there is a claim. That puts a automatic w/v thunderdome that may be quick to resolve (see below).

I think at the end of the day we should also claim night given that scum may want to sneak in a N3 vig fakeclaim or something with much less heat just so they get into the auto-towncore.

How to deal with CC's:

Scum fakeclaiming N1 vig is self-resolvable for obvious reasons, Scum fakeclaiming N2 vig is a situation where we hang outside of the two "vigs", let N1 be N1, hang outside of the two "vigs" again (I think?) and then let the claims resolve during the night. N3 vig CC is weird given that we aren't automatically going to have a N3 so probably let N1 vig shoot scummier claim and then hang the other CC tmrw if that doesn't work.


ok now time to read

N1 Vig should always shoot imo given that if the VT's and the vig's make a decent PoE then scum will be on their feet for today. We kinda want that pressure and even if the N1 Vig thinks he is bad at shooting and stuff just make a rand shot in the PoE.

Still questioning to whether all Vig's should claim. I feel like it's a little too early to let them claim now for sure? Maybe if we let them all claim at the end of the day to spread scum's kill potential out and make sure the N1 doesn't randomly shoot a vig in PoE. Clearly the named townies are a boost but claiming right at RVS start is kinda bruh because that basically sets scum's kill potential on the vig's only and not on fearkills or anything else. I think we still claim vig today given that named townies could really help us in future days (scum can only kill 1 vig n1) but if someone like Osmium gets good reads and has massive fearkill potential then we can capitalize on that at the end of the day.

Counterclaims:

Clearly if you are under pressure like L-1 on your wagon you should claim with your night if you are a vig. That allows potential CC to happen.

Do not be afraid to counterclaim with your night if there is a claim. That puts a automatic w/v thunderdome that may be quick to resolve (see below).

I think at the end of the day we should also claim night given that scum may want to sneak in a N3 vig fakeclaim or something with much less heat just so they get into the auto-towncore.

How to deal with CC's:

Scum fakeclaiming N1 vig is self-resolvable for obvious reasons, Scum fakeclaiming N2 vig is a situation where we hang outside of the two "vigs", let N1 be N1, hang outside of the two "vigs" again (I think?) and then let the claims resolve during the night. N3 vig CC is weird given that we aren't automatically going to have a N3 so probably let N1 vig shoot scummier claim and then hang the other CC tmrw if that doesn't work.

post_rendered:
You asked for it.

Baseline knowledge for everyone here is that N1 vig CC's are completely self-resolvable as vig can shoot the fakeclaim during the night. If you didn't know this you should spend more time thinking about setup knowing what's right and wrong.

Bob's plan is this:
BobDBuilder321 wrote:
Ok, so majority seems to agree with me.

We essentially have 2 hangs everyday. So everyone will vote for who they think is scum, but they'll also have a secondary hang. We'll do a boatcount then, for who should be vigged. So we hang whoever has the most votes for the hang and vig whoever has the most votes in the boatcount.

Exactly 24 hours before EOD, the person winning the boatcount will claim. If they claim VT, we vig them. If they claim vig (with night), we wait for ccs, and if there are none, then we quickly decide on another vigshot. If there's a cc we decide who's scum and who's vig UNLESS there's a N1 vig cc, in which case we just do nothing and let mafia and vig kill each other.

At MYLO/LYLO, all the vigs remaining claim.

Now, I think it fails at the point where either the player winning the boatcount is inactive 24 hours before EoD (which I could see happening) and just doesn't claim or the player winning the boatcount fakeclaims N1 vig (without us knowing) while the actual N1 vig is inactive and doesn't CC.
(or I guess if scum is confident enough they could claim N3 vig in hopes of being townier than the actual vig claim and survive another day)

N1 vig claiming now doesn't actually hurt us much, as long as if the N1 vig is fake then there is a CC which is self-resolvable, and otherwise if we have just one N1 vig claim then that person is automatically conftown. We can basically build off of that one N1 vig claim as our towncore and instead of making a separate "boatcount" for vig claims which can be heavily scum-influenced just create a strong PoE, get the other vig's to claim EoD if they are stil in PoE, and shoot inside PoE.

How to deal with EoD CC's:
EulerNTM wrote:
How to deal with CC's:

Scum fakeclaiming N1 vig is self-resolvable for obvious reasons, Scum fakeclaiming N2 vig is a situation where we hang outside of the two "vigs", let N1 be N1, hang outside of the two "vigs" again (I think?) and then let the claims resolve during the night. N3 vig CC is weird given that we aren't automatically going to have a N3 so probably let N1 vig shoot scummier claim and then hang the other CC tmrw if that doesn't work.

I'm not exactly a full supporter of N1 vig claiming but it's fine with me given that we either hit scum tonight or we have a conftown today. I think it is better than the plan that Bob had in mind.
post_canonical:
You asked for it.

Baseline knowledge for everyone here is that N1 vig CC's are completely self-resolvable as vig can shoot the fakeclaim during the night. If you didn't know this you should spend more time thinking about setup knowing what's right and wrong.

Bob's plan is this:

[quote=BobDBuilder321]Ok, so majority seems to agree with me.

We essentially have 2 hangs everyday. So everyone will vote for who they think is scum, but they'll also have a secondary hang. We'll do a boatcount then, for who should be vigged. So we hang whoever has the most votes for the hang and vig whoever has the most votes in the boatcount.

Exactly 24 hours before EOD, the person winning the boatcount will claim. If they claim VT, we vig them. If they claim vig (with night), we wait for ccs, and if there are none, then we quickly decide on another vigshot. If there's a cc we decide who's scum and who's vig UNLESS there's a N1 vig cc, in which case we just do nothing and let mafia and vig kill each other.

At MYLO/LYLO, all the vigs remaining claim. 
[/quote]

Now, I think it fails at the point where either the player winning the boatcount is inactive 24 hours before EoD (which I could see happening) and just doesn't claim or the player winning the boatcount fakeclaims N1 vig (without us knowing) while the actual N1 vig is inactive and doesn't CC. 
(or I guess if scum is confident enough they could claim N3 vig in hopes of being townier than the actual vig claim and survive another day)

N1 vig claiming now doesn't actually hurt us much, as long as if the N1 vig is fake then there is a CC which is self-resolvable, and otherwise if we have just one N1 vig claim then that person is automatically conftown. We can basically build off of that one N1 vig claim as our towncore and instead of making a separate "boatcount" for vig claims which can be heavily scum-influenced just create a strong PoE, get the other vig's to claim EoD if they are stil in PoE, and shoot inside PoE.

How to deal with EoD CC's:

[quote name="EulerNTM" url="/community/p20309705"]
[size=150][b]How to deal with CC's:[/b][/size]

Scum fakeclaiming N1 vig is self-resolvable for obvious reasons, Scum fakeclaiming N2 vig is a situation where we hang outside of the two "vigs", let N1 be N1, hang outside of the two "vigs" again (I think?) and then let the claims resolve during the night. N3 vig CC is weird given that we aren't automatically going to have a N3 so probably let N1 vig shoot scummier claim and then hang the other CC tmrw if that doesn't work.
[/quote]

I'm not exactly a full supporter of N1 vig claiming but it's fine with me given that we either hit scum tonight or we have a conftown today. I think it is better than the plan that Bob had in mind.

post_rendered:
Final wallpost I'm ever making about setup spec.

large wall
So, we already outed midway as the N1 Vig (I think) and from what I hear we have to give midway a specific target to shoot because we really can't trust him with his Vig ability.

We should probably just decide on one person for midway to shoot well before deadline for claiming procedures and it should be a unresolvable slot imo so that it's not a scum pushed mis-shot. That should be the best we can do with midway being N1 vig.

Now I think that we shouldn't get the other vig's to claim and trust them to not be in the PoE because if N2 and N3 Vigs stay hidden then we may be able to get another town controlled kill from one of the other vigs. Obviously we will still have to get the person that we select midway to shoot to claim in order to prevent midway shooting the N2 or N3 vigs but as long as we select our target a couple days beforehand that shouldn't be too much of a problem.

The boating idea is still bad for 2 reasons. First being that a "hang" system is much easier to be scum-influenced because it's practically a second town hang and if scum is decently competent then they can drive 2 mishangs today. However, PoE is much harder for scum to change and influence so I think there actually is a better chance if we let the vigs shoot who they think is scummiest in PoE. The vig judgement will not be altered with the goal of mis-shooting a townie, it will be aiming to kill scum in PoE.
Secondly, there is the activity issue. Boating requires the leading wagon to claim 24 hours before EoD where it's uncertain that said leading wagon will claim in time, but if we construct a sturdy PoE a couple days before deadline then if a Vig somehow ends up inside the PoE they can claim. Of course, the whole goal of a PoE is for the vigs to be towny enough not to be in the PoE in the first place so this shouldn't happen with our method, but even if it does the vigs have ample time to claim.


ok now to actually focus on AI stuff

Warning: Effort post incoming.

Alright, it's time to actually move out of this vig spec dumpster fire and actually scumhunt. Here are my reads on the entire playerlist with a pretty thorough explanation, and feel free to contest any of them.
1. Scipio1 - Townlean
Gut tells me that Scipio is town.

2. EulerNTM - Townread
His posts actually have content, which tells me that he's town. If you go back and look at 217, Euler's posts were basically just restating the same thing over and over again. For the past couple of games where Euler's been town, his posts are long and tryhardy filled with content which makes me pretty confident that he's town.

3. nikenissan - Null
Liked his entrance but still not enough info either way to make a judgement.

4. Osmium_ - Townread
Osmium is hard to read but all his posts have been very towny. I also like his reaction to being conftowned by Snoozing. The fact that he exists is enough so townread.

5. cherryolive - Scumlean
Weird opening. Cherry sidelined themselves during the vig argument and instead chose to focus on whether the argument was v/v. Then when I made an offhand comment on a scumlean on Cherry they chose to make two separate posts defending against a nonexistent attack, the second of which came way after my original post. Overall doesn't have much of a presence in the game. My gut tells me that they are town but my brain says scum, so I'm going with scumlean for now.

6. midwaybear - Townlean
Seems to be genuinely concerned for the welfare of town when proposing the "all vigs claim" plan. Avid participant in the debate as well. I won't pass judgement on whether he is a vig or not. Proposing a PL on Bob in the first day of D1? Borderline TWTBAW, and lines up with his very strange town meta perfectly.

7. BobDBuilder321 - Townread
Seems town and lines up pretty much perfectly with what he did in 218.

8. cw357 - Scumlean
Cw is giving me weird vibes. I mean, this argument normally wouldn't be valid for anyone other than Cw, but I thought Cw was a lot less... serious than this. Like doesn't he usually troll way more? From my experience with him in 218 and 223, he generally starts deathtunneling people all over the place and is generally loud but unproductive. Here he just seems a lot more coasty. He is still making spammy posts and stuff but it just seems toned down A LOT. Personally, I just feel like he has a lot less presence in the game and that concerns me.

9. snoozingnewt - Townread
So if you scroll up to the very beginning, when the Bob vs. Midway+Osmium debate started, Snoozing seemed kinda indecisive. He went back and forth a bit before going with Bob which seems to me to be pretty towny. I feel like scum would not take the time to do that and even if they did, they'd probably side with Midway+Osmium. Snoozing also had a lot of posts that seemed to contribute by giving his own opinion which you see not a lot of in 223. The only doubt I have about this townread is the fact that some of Snoozing's posts seem like fillers, which he did a lot of in 223. Granted, in this game he posts a lot less of it, but it still makes me a bit paranoid. But I think town!Snoozing also posts some of those kinds of posts so I'm sticking with the townread here.

10. hannahptl - Scumlean
Seems to be sidelining herself a lot and generally not making much contribution. Haven't seen anything overtly scummy but for the most part she avoided the vig spec debate which seems to me like distancing from controversial issues to get out of the spotlight.

11. bigmath - Null
Not enough info.

12. Socoobo - Conftown
Conftown. Need I say more? Actually, that was a rhetorical question. The answer is no.
My PoE is then {Scipio1, nikenissan, cherryolive, midwaybear, cw357, hannahptl, bigmath}. I know it's pretty big but meh.

I am okay with hanging anyone in {hannahptl, cw357, cherryolive} as of right now.

post_rendered:
Did we ever come to an agreement about the vig claims? Has anyone seriously claimed vig?

If they claim today:
D1 - we have 3 confirmed town
N1 - we get the extra kill, N2 vig gets killed by mafia
N2 - N3 vig gets killed by mafia
Here we lose 2 of our extra hangs for 2 confirmed town. That doesn't seem like a good tradeoff to me.

If they claim on the day before their kill:
D1 - we have 1 confirmed town
N1 - we have 1 extra hang
D2 - we have 1 confirmed town
N2 - we have 1 extra hang
D3 - we have 1 confirmed town
N3 - we have 1 extra hang
That's assuming mafia go for the confirmed town, rather than take their chances at hitting vig.
If the chosen vig kill claims vig, we wait for a counter-claim. Correct me if I'm wrong, but I don't think it's optimal for mafia to counter-claim here, so we probably won't get one. If we don't get a counter-claim, we choose someone else. If we do, we can choose 1 of the vig claimers to kill, so we'll have at least a half chance of hitting mafia.
So, waiting on the vig claims seems like a better plan to me.

So Post #37 is where I first suggested my plan that vigs shouldn't claim at all, town decides who vig shoots, blah blah blah.

Post #38, Scipio says/agrees that all vigs should claim without nights.

Post #78, bigmath says he doesn't think any of the plans are good.

Post #104 I posted my plan again, except making it more readable and easy to understand this time.

Post #112, Socoobo says he sides with me in the vigs-claim-or-not-claim debate. He elaborates that "claiming vig outright so early will just make us lose PRs unnecessarily.". Nothing about N1 vig should claim.

Post #115, Socoobo says he agrees with my plan, which was no vigs claim at all D1.

Post #118, Scipio says he's ok with either plan.

Post #119, bigmath says he agrees with my plan.

Post #122, snoozing says he agrees with my plan.

Post #146, Scipio cites a concern about my plan (which I do address).

Post #168, Socoobo says my plan is the best, then midway's, then Osmium's.

Post #175 Socoobo changes his opinion and says N1 vig claiming is fine, and is the first one to switch from my plan to N1 vig claiming plan.

Post #178, bigmath cites a concern with the plan (which was addressed at some point in the game).

Post #196, bigmath changes his mind about some parts of my plan, but not the claiming part.

Post #199, Euler says that it's too early to let vigs claim now, but it might be good to let then claim at the end of the day.

Post #212, cherry says they're not choosing a side.

Post #213, Euler floats the idea of all vigs claim without nights.

Post #215, Euler says vigs should not claim now, that's worse than claiming EOD. He also says that he doesn't see how vigs dying hurts town much, contrary to what I was saying.

Post #216, cherry suggests that vigs claim after their kills.

Post #231, cherry suggests that N1 and N2 vig claim after N2. They also say "I'm just dumb. Please don't mind me." which implies they're not very sure if they have a good plan or not.

Post #238, Euler agrees with me that we should ignore Osmium and midway saying vig should claim rn.

Post #239, Socoobo doubles down on his belief that N1 vig should claim.

Post #251, cw says N1 vig should claim.

Post #254, Socoobo defends the N1 vig should claim strat.

Post [url]=https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/c49h2446025p20310953[#270/url], cherry says they're against N1 vig claiming.

Post [/url]#274, snoozing says "My opinion is that it depends on who n1 vig is but since half the game claimed not n1 vig already i'd say no because it looks like all the competent players already claimed not n1 vig and having conftown incompetent doesn't matter unless we're about to hang them", and I'm not sure what he's trying to say.

Post #275, bigmath claims that N1 vig claiming is better and my plan "falls apart after inspecting". (he doesn't elaborate how).

Post #277, Euler says that there should be a massclaim eod.

Post #280, Euler says that he won't be against fully claiming now when he finds what's wrong with my plan.

Post #283, cherry expresses their support for N1 vig claiming.

Post #289, snoozing implies that he changed to N1 vig should claim, which I think means that #274 was where snoozing switched?

Ok that's all, time to analyze.


I didn't include Osmium or midway because their opinions barely changed.

post_rendered:
Quote:
we get an easy conftown, and if mafia kills him/her off then there's no downside because they have already used their PR
The downside is that we don't get a conftown at the ideal time. N1 vig is basically an inno child. An inno child does not claim D1--they claim at the optimal time to have a conf town.
Quote:
ALSO, it's even more beneficial now that we know who N2 vig is, because now mafia will be forced to decide who to kill on N1, the conftown or the N2 vig
Why would we want to give mafia more choices? That's like purposely dropping a piece in chess when you're about to lose a piece so "the other guy has to choose which piece to take". It makes no sense.
Quote:
Either way, we get a conftown for the entirety of D2
An inno child should claim at the ideal time to have a conftown, not any random day. A one-shot vig who used their shot is basically an inno child, so the same logic applies here.
Quote:
or we get the N2 kill which could possibly flip red.
We get the N2 kill without claiming. Look, if N2 vig doesn't claim, we get an extra hang and a conftown. If N2 vig does claim, we get a conftown(which is useless in this case anyway) but no extra hang. Clearly, not claiming is better.

post_rendered:
"Hello, I want you to hack into Suspectum Industries and remove the payroll information. And make sure you do it before tomorrow."
"Before tomorrow??"
"Yes. It is crucial."


Normally everyone woke up as late as they possibly could. But not today, since payday had come. At 6:00 AM, someone woke up and checked their bank account for their pay. It hadn't come yet. Confused, they checked the payroll. It was blank.

Two people heard a ping and checked their phones at the same time. Messages from the board officials.
"Due to unknown reasons, you have been fired."
EulerNTM was fired(killed). They were the Second Board Official (N2 Vigilante).
cw357 was fired(killed). They were a Manager (Vanilla Townie).


Day 2 begins now, and deadline is 4:30 pm Central, on March 2nd.
There are currently 9 alive.

post_rendered:
Okay, this game wasn't exactly a big offender in this regard, but it sure made the problem clear. Been wanting to scream about this recently.

You know all those times you resign to the fate of "PoE hangs" by saying "This guy will never get out of PoE so we should just kill him"? Makes sense, right? Your win condition is to narrow PoE, right?

No, your win condition is to kill scum. Kill. Scum.

First of all, while there is such a thing as a truly unresolvable slot, chances are only one or two of the slots you've ever called unresolvable have actually been unresolvable. The other cases? You're just lazy. Or tunneled. Or both. This game saw just that, with early acceptance of the deaths of two townies, one who barely registered as scummy for oddly placed lolcatting a couple of times at worst, and one who was killed for being off-meta. These would be acceptable arguments if they were the results of examining the entire playerlist and finding the others townier, but they most definitely weren't.

And guess what? If you were looking for reasons not to hang people as well as reasons to hang people, you could've looked closer and seen that nike was obvtown. He had genuinely no idea what was going on, he knew he was probably screwed, and he was still trying his absolute hardest to somehow help town in any way possible, even when he couldn’t tell what that would even mean. The extremes of purity and WIM under the most extreme pressure. He broke at a time where it was more than reasonable to break.

Second of all, even if you think you have a winning PoE, you should still aim for accuracy. You will make wrong PoEs. That is a fact of life. That is inevitable. You shouldn't just stop all work the moment you think you've made it.

If you catch and kill scum, you can remove more townies from PoE by interactions. If you truly care to play the game even after you think you've won by PoE, you'll probably clear even more people just in discussion. Think of all the clears you could access just by deciding to play.

If you have a wrong PoE of size 4 in a 13-player game, you'll find out that it's wrong in F5, and you'll have two chances to fix it. If you have a wrong PoE of size 5 in a 13-player game, you'll find out that it's wrong in F3, and you'll have one chance to fix it. If you have a wrong PoE of size 6 in a 13-player game, too late GG you're dead. The narrower you get PoE, the sooner you'll realize it's wrong, if it is, and the more time you'll have to correct it.

All of this is to say that "but we have to" hangs are bad even when you think you have the game locked down. Except, in the case of cw, you didn't already have a winning PoE. You said "Hey, in the worst case, this can't make our jobs any harder, right? We'd never be able to clear this guy anyway." No, it made your jobs harder. You could've flipped a wolf that would clear cw (and hptl would’ve cleared cw). You could've looked at more than just stylistic elements of cw's play. You could've looked through a hundred ways of reading cw. You cannot decide on D1 that anyone actually actively competently engaging the game will be permanently unresolvable. Under no circumstances.

Anyway, this game wasn’t all bad, of course. It actually increased the number of people I’ll harass to apply for stuff.
post_canonical:
Okay, this game wasn't exactly a big offender in this regard, but it sure made the problem clear. Been wanting to scream about this recently.

You know all those times you resign to the fate of "PoE hangs" by saying "This guy will never get out of PoE so we should just kill him"? Makes sense, right? Your win condition is to narrow PoE, right?

No, your win condition is to kill scum. Kill. Scum.

First of all, while there is such a thing as a truly unresolvable slot, chances are only one or two of the slots you've ever called unresolvable have actually been unresolvable. The other cases? You're just lazy. Or tunneled. Or both. This game saw just that, with early acceptance of the deaths of two townies, one who barely registered as scummy for oddly placed lolcatting a couple of times at worst, and one who was killed for being off-meta. These would be acceptable arguments if they were the results of examining the entire playerlist and finding the others townier, but they most definitely weren't.

And guess what? If you were looking for reasons not to hang people as well as reasons to hang people, you could've looked closer and seen that nike was obvtown. He had genuinely no idea what was going on, he knew he was probably screwed, and he was still trying his absolute hardest to somehow help town in any way possible, even when he couldn’t tell what that would even mean. The extremes of purity and WIM under the most extreme pressure. He broke at a time where it was more than reasonable to break.

Second of all, even if you think you have a winning PoE, you should still aim for accuracy. You will make wrong PoEs. That is a fact of life. That is inevitable. You shouldn't just stop all work the moment you think you've made it.

If you catch and kill scum, you can remove more townies from PoE by interactions. If you truly care to play the game even after you think you've won by PoE, you'll probably clear even more people just in discussion. Think of all the clears you could access just by deciding to play.

If you have a wrong PoE of size 4 in a 13-player game, you'll find out that it's wrong in F5, and you'll have two chances to fix it. If you have a wrong PoE of size 5 in a 13-player game, you'll find out that it's wrong in F3, and you'll have one chance to fix it. If you have a wrong PoE of size 6 in a 13-player game, too late GG you're dead. The narrower you get PoE, the sooner you'll realize it's wrong, if it is, and the more time you'll have to correct it.

All of this is to say that "but we have to" hangs are bad even when you think you have the game locked down. Except, in the case of cw, you didn't already have a winning PoE. You said "Hey, in the worst case, this can't make our jobs any harder, right? We'd never be able to clear this guy anyway." No, it made your jobs harder. You could've flipped a wolf that would clear cw ([i]and hptl would’ve cleared cw[/i]). You could've looked at more than just stylistic elements of cw's play. You could've looked through a hundred ways of reading cw. You cannot decide on D1 that anyone actually actively competently engaging the game will be permanently unresolvable. Under no circumstances.

Some Thoughts/Feedback that is both probably biased and in inconsistent amounts:
On Setup
For this Setup, the EV (expected value of random rational actions) for town of a massclaim day 1 is 5/21. This should be rather clearly the lowest EV of all reasonable strategies. (An unreasonable strategy example would be hanging day 1 a N1 vig claim.) The power of this setup came from 3 named townies, a free vigshot, and a tiny chance at getting an extra town controlled kill by getting off all three vigshots.
-Named townies don't want to roleclaim unless their gonna die otherwise or mass.
-The free vigshot doesn't affect the need of vigi's claiming.
-To get the extra town controlled kill the N2 and N3 vigilantes need to stay hidden.

Basically what I'm saying is I don't understand at all why you guys massclaimed.
Midway's proposed strategy was not much better.
Bob's strategy is interesting, definitely better, but don't think it's quite optimal (it might be though).
Pretty sure (though not fully) the optimal strategy is to treat the pr's like you would in matrix 6 etc and only claim if you're gonna die or mass claim.

On Day 1
Overall day 1 was solid from both teams. Multiple scum were leading the votecount throughout most of the day, though the scumteam did well making sure there wasn't much of an actual risk.

Nike had good reads this game but still needs to get more persuasive and townier.

On Night 1
I guess I had thought that this had been cw's best game so far, so I was kinda surprised when he reentered into the hang pool straight to the vigshot. Playwise nothing wrong with it.

Euler claiming his role ensured his death. Really wasn't necessary, if a scum claimed your role while you were gone you just shoot them night 2.

On Day 2
This was a good day for town. cherry and hannahptl were getting associated even before ptl went down. Snoozing though was still in a great position.

hannahptl played fine, definitely would really recommend actually reading who your partners are day 1, since not doing so is really leaving things to chance.

On Night 2
Rather straightforward kill for the scumteam. Osmium_ played good, from moderator pov I could tell he was trying to not get nk'd during day 1.

On Day 3
This quickhang was uh rather disrespectful to ming, socoobo, and vku.
Very beneficial to the scumteam.

Ming replaced in to a scumread slot and wasn't able to turn it around, especially with the quickhang.

On Night 3
More than fine for scumteam to not kill the last vig yet. (With hindsight they probably should've but hindsight 20/20)

I assume Scipio played pretty well considering he was nk'd over a clear, kinda blanking sorry. Acting like a vigi when people were trying to get them to claim was good play.

On Day 4
Bob Bob Bob Bob Bob
A hero or a lucky fool?
Questions I ask myself.

cherryolive lived longer than I expected them to based off the start of the game, mostly was from good play. Though you really fell apart here and near eod2.

Night 4
Socoobo you came off as a background figure / sideline / whatever the term is, you could probably benefit from playing a more central role in the early game, though I'm not sure how one makes that kind of playstyle change. (Might have been circumstance instead of playstyle, this is my first game seeing you play.)

Granted you ended up getting cleared some how (I don't remember when) so the above is probably bad advice.

Day 5
Destiny. Snoozing you played well throughout the game. I'm not sure what step you need to take to convert these 3p lylo's into wins.

Bob came off as the most rational person during early day 1, later day 1 was ehh. Day 2 you were still onto snoozing which was good but were townreading the other two scum so kinda balances out. Day3+ was kinda a rollercoaster.

VKU427 you were a universal townlean since replacing in, and pretty much coasted off that. (At least that's how I recall it, could be wrong.)


Overall am pleased with both the setup and the game.
Also didn't run into any moderator issues beyond like two edited posts which was nice.


Also general statement for future, please don't try to replace out if your only reason is you think your losing or you think someone else would mildly better help your team. Had this problem some here and 207 and as seen in 207 it really makes the game drag. (Of course replacing out because you're busier and can't spend as much time on the game or etc is still more than fine.)
